---
title: "UIAO Modernization Master Plan"
subtitle: "Five-document program baseline: Phase 0 → Phase 1 → Boundary → Compliance → 90-Day Execution"
doc-type: section-landing
audience: "CIO · CISO · ISSO · Authorizing Official (AO) · 3PAO · Program leadership"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
date: 2026-04-26
---

# UIAO Modernization Master Plan {.unnumbered}

The Master Plan is a five-document program baseline for the Unified
Identity–Addressing–Overlay Architecture (UIAO) modernization program.
The five documents form a strict dependency chain: each is the upstream
source for the next, and no downstream artifact is ratified against an
unratified upstream baseline.

## Core Series (read in order)

| # | Document | Role |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | [Phase 0 — Planning Document](phase-0-planning.qmd) | Scope, planes, FedRAMP Boundary Gap, objectives, deliverables, risks |
| 2 | [Phase 1 — Architecture Document](phase-1-architecture.qmd) | Plane model, interfaces, signal taxonomy, control bindings, conformance |
| 3 | [Boundary Modernization Appendix](boundary-modernization-appendix.qmd) | Boundary class taxonomy, continuity matrix, Signal Restoration Framework |
| 4 | [Compliance & ATO Alignment](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd) | Mandate landscape, KSI derivation, evidence chain, cATO operating model |
| 5 | [90-Day Execution Plan](90-day-execution-plan.qmd) | Sequencing principles, three thirty-day windows, conformance gates |

## Companion Documents (read alongside)

| Document | Role |
|---|---|
| [Executive Brief](executive-brief.qmd) | One-page condensed brief for CIO / CISO / AO sign-off; cites upstream sections for depth |
| [Glossary](glossary.qmd) | Acronyms, mandate references, and key concepts used across the series |

## Reading Path

The five documents are written to be read in numbered order. Two
shorter reading paths are also valid:

- **AO / 3PAO short path** — Phase 0 §1, Phase 0 §4, Compliance §5,
  Compliance §7, 90-Day §5.3. This path covers the federal alignment,
  the structural dependency, the cATO operating model, and the program
  exit criteria.
- **Engineering short path** — Phase 1 §2 through §7, Boundary
  Appendix §3 and §4, 90-Day §3 through §5. This path covers the
  architectural model, the boundary redesign, and the execution
  sequence.

## Dependency Chain

```
   Phase 0 Planning
        │
        ▼
   Phase 1 Architecture
        │
        ▼
   Boundary Modernization Appendix
        │
        ▼
   Compliance & ATO Alignment
        │
        ▼
   90-Day Execution Plan
        │
        ▼
   Sustained cATO Operations
```

Sequencing is enforced by the canon-change protocol: a downstream
document may not be ratified, and downstream execution may not begin,
until its named upstream baseline is in place. This rule is restated in
each document's *Hand-off* section.

## Federal Alignment

The Master Plan is framed as direct alignment with the existing federal
mandate landscape, not a net-new initiative. The full mandate set is
enumerated in [Compliance & ATO Alignment §1](compliance-and-ato-alignment.qmd#mandate-landscape):

- Executive Order 14028
- OMB M-22-09 and OMB M-24-15
- NIST SP 800-207 (Zero Trust Architecture)
- NIST SP 800-53 Rev 5
- FedRAMP Rev 5
- CISA SCuBA
- NIST OSCAL

## Audience

The Master Plan is written for CIO, CISO, ISSO, Authorizing Official,
and 3PAO audiences. Engineering leads and program management consume
the same documents; reading depth varies by section, not by audience.

## Governance

All five documents are canonical program artifacts governed by the
canon-change protocol. Substantive changes require an ADR, a schema
check, and a drift scan. Derived consumers (per-adapter design,
operational runbooks, training materials) cite these documents as their
upstream source.
